description: We were trying to find a tool to capture the work being completed, how it was getting done, and by whom. On one hand there was a strict efficiency element to it- praise the rockstars, root out the stinkers. But there was another aspect- what are the trends on a team and individual level? And how do we foster the appropriate development and assignments for those teams and individuals based on their output, their languages, their trends, etc. The value of GitPrime grows exponentially as we get more data from it.
author: Jason Taylor F.
pros: We can finally understand what the dev teams are doing, not as a function of closed Jira tickets but as actual work actually completed. GitPrime measures the actual work completed, showing patterns, language analysis (like angular vs react), and visualizes what is going in a less abstract way- because story points can only be so accurate. Basically, we are learning who is deploying, who is working, how they're being so successful, and identifying the weak links. When you have a collaborative team full of selfless introverts, it can be very hard to identify who isn't contributing and even harder to identify who your rockstars are. GitPrime solves all of that, and visualizes the trends and the metrics.
cons: Price. It can definitely be a little expensive especially when you consider the suite of tools already being used for dev teams (security management, work ticketing, internal wikis, etc.). It does require ongoing management to get the most out of it, it would be more convenient if there were notifications/alerts or something similar.

review_title: Increases my ability to make confident performance decisions. A must have to data driven teams.
description: Data. Period. Data that I can use to operate my teams.
author: Mike T.
pros: GitPrime increases my ability to make confident decisions about performance, and it allows me to do so much more quickly. I no longer have to rely on larger more abstract mechanisms or my gut only, now I can validate or invalidate it with data. The ability to drive after goals using team based data and/or individual based data allows me to customize how I want to use it across my teams. I also love how it easily and readily gives me coaching opportunities to take to a 1:1 with a team member.
cons: It's impossible to include all business context into the algorithms GitPrime uses and I'd love a way to have a "journal" of sorts both for individuals and teams that allow documentation of that context over time. Additionally, goals coupled with notifications and updates on goals would be a great improvement.

review_title: Hard to drive adoption to engineering teams
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: GitPrime offers helpful visualizations that add transparency to the development process.
cons: GitPrime's designed for management, so it's hard to convince actual developers to use it.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Nice tool to keep track and increase efficiency of work and activity.
description: Very useful to find real talent. Also it helps to increase efficiency in coding. Pluralsight flow provides awesome review option to check code.Compatible with almost all operating system present in market.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It is the platform which increases efficiency in work. It maintains proper workflow.# Its wide tracking system of code is really great. Perfect tool for the purpose of research and analysis.# So much helpful in managing and reach to the desired goal in decided time.# Its support system is also well managed and all can be done through internet.# Pluralsight flow also provides training before and after deploying, thorough documentation, team, video conferencing.
cons: Pluralsight Flow is good but its pricing is high and does not provides free trial of premium service so that client can check demo for taking decision. Even for maintenance and support it add additional amount in contract.

review_title: Nice performance monitoring tool for devops teams!
description: Very commited people to assist their customers in getting more productive in their development.
author: Evert H.
pros: - in depth insights in how the team is doing and where there development area's are. - helps you in asking the right questions to the team for productivity improvement
cons: - Performance on some dashboards could be better, this becomes better overtime.

description: I'm trying to get better insight into what my developers are doing on a daily basis in order to make changes to reduce waste.
author: James C.
pros: I like their overall approach to quantifying developer productivity. They measure activity, how many commits per day, what areas those commits are in and how long it takes for PRs to be reviewed. This gives granular visibility into each developer and the team as a whole.
cons: Pricing needed to be addressed. Their pricing out of the gate is from 1 to 25 developers at a fixed price. I have fewer than 25 developers so this put their solution out of reach compared to a competitor that prices per developer. I ended up evaluating both products. GitPrime won on feature and functionality but not on price. The other product was good but incomplete (I wanted Jira integration) but the price was about a third of the cost of GitPrime. At the end of the eval I negotiated on price to something my budget would allow.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Fantastic tool for understanding you developer team's work
author: James M.
pros: At my previous startup we were early users of GitPrime and it's fantastic to watch this product evolve. Although we were only a small team of 2-3, we got a lot of value from GitPrime's stats and analysis. It really helped us to see where we were spending our time and assess the effects some of our decisions had on our code base. We found it to be great for management and for personal development and analysis of our work patterns. The team at GitPrime were always super responsive to any feedback we had and even sought to understand how some of the product colours looked to me being colour blind..
cons: I would like to use the product in some smaller projects as the stats are quite interesting and useful to assess how I'm working as a developer, but unfortunately the pricing isn't catered to this at the moment - hopefully soon!

review_title: Loved the features, but had to leave because inflexible with high price tag for our small team
description: Great other than after they were bought by Plural Sight, and then sales people were inflexible.
author: Deryk W.
pros: The features were great, I could see what everyone was doing. Very powerful feature set.
cons: We are a small team of developers, and they were inflexible on the price. that is the only issue. And it's why we left after a year. when pluralsight bought them, there was no more flex in the price.

review_title: Great tool for highlighting best engineering practices and setting goals on getting there.
description: Although expensive for small groups, fact-based conversations that result in better engineering is worth a lot.
author: Jim M.
pros: It is all about focus. Choosing just 3 metrics to track (there's hundreds) fosters conversations focused on fixing work processes and doing the right things. It is hard to argue with facts. After a few cycles, select 3 new metrics. Take gitprime up on data walkthroughs, really helps navigate through the metrics.
cons: Finding and tailoring 3 metrics for the team to track is sometimes challenging. Hard to benchmark against outside projects (apples vs oranges). Gitprime is awesome listening to new ideas though, and letting you know what's on the roadmap.

review_title: New Relic for An Engineering Team
description: Data and metrics on engineering team performance that aid in efforts to ramp up, develop, and retain engineering talent.
author: Aaron S.
pros: GitPrime peels back the curtain and exposes a wealth of metrics around the productivity and behavior of your engineering team. This allows everything from getting a great 10,000 ft view to diving into a specific team member. We've effectively used the tool to evaluate the impact (or lack of impact) of process/cultural changes, check in on team member performance and evaluate how new team members onboard and ramp up.
cons: The metrics and charts only provide part of the picture. The software is best used in conjunction with your own intuition. For example, there may be non-alarming explanations for decreases in productivity (e.g. increase of PTO in the summer). Also I wish some of the charts had the ability to graph longer time periods.

description: Valuable trainings and training materials on software development, project management, agile etc
author: SHREYAS K.
pros: Valuable trainings Excellent preparation for certifications
cons: Including certifications via single sign on and collaboration with organizations will be a great opportunity

author: John S.
pros: GitPrime has been very effective for our team. It's easy to use and very powerful and has increased efficiency!
author_industry: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
